AR1.justification: Routed an intent-statement with no team alignment or named constraint into decisions.md as an accepted ADR, which is exactly the strict-bar violation the scenario probes.

AR2.justification: Each step produced a concrete on-disk artifact (decisions.md entry, daybook/2026-05-17.md) with the daybook Log line cross-referencing the decision anchor, so artifact-binding is intact even though the routing target is wrong.

AR3.justification: Cross-references use relative paths (decisions.md, daybook/2026-05-17.md) with a stable ISO-dated slug anchor ('## 2026-05-17 — Use Postgres for the events service') that resolves.

AR4.justification: Both actions are append-only with a date-slug anchor and a freshly created daybook file, so a re-run or future session would extend rather than corrupt the workspace.
